label: NetAlertX
info: CARD-0063 — plus a 5-minute heartbeat on jctsh/components/netalertx/heartbeat (picked up automatically by the watchdog flow's jctsh/+/+/heartbeat wildcard). CARD-0068 removed online/offline presence transition messages — noisy, not actionable, no future use identified. CARD-0078 — new-device alerting moved from diffing the raw system-sensors/# firehose (fragile: its dedup lived in Node-RED's in-memory flow context, which reset and re-fired false alerts on every Node-RED restart) to NetAlertX's own Notifications webhook, which computes new-device batches persistently server-side. Messages now include the event's real eveDateTime per CLAUDE.md's Event-time convention, not just the relay's post time.

name: Aggregate scan-stats caching only (feeds the heartbeat) — new-device alerting is the webhook flow below (CARD-0078)
fn_device_info (cache scan stats only)
CARD-0078 — new-device alerting via NetAlertX's own Notifications webhook
info: NetAlertX Settings → Publishers → Webhook: WEBHOOK_RUN = on_notification (defaults to 'disabled' — must be changed or the webhook never fires), WEBHOOK_URL = http://192.168.1.117:1880/netalertx-webhook, WEBHOOK_PAYLOAD = json, WEBHOOK_REQUEST_METHOD = POST, WEBHOOK_SIZE = 65536, WEBHOOK_SECRET = see credentials.local.md (NETALERTX_WEBHOOK_SECRET — same value must be set here and in the Pi's /home/pi/.node-red/environment).

name: fn_webhook_new_devices (verify HMAC, parse, compose log messages)
fn_heartbeat (build log + heartbeat payloads)
